subject: Lane AH-002 — failure propagation dedup blocked by AH-001
body: {"lane_id":"AH-002","blocked_at":"execution","finding":"Test 2 (compute failure propagation) reveals the race condition AH-001 is fixing. When compute fails via Effect.die, the inflight dedup doesn't work — all 10 concurrently waiting fibers independently run compute instead of deduping. The success case (Test 1) passes because synchronous compute completes before other fibers can race. Root cause: the Deferred in cache.ts has error channel 'never' but is completed with failure exits via 'as any' cast, which causes Deferred.await to produce a defect instead of propagating the failure.","options":[{"id":"keep-test-expecting-correct-behavior","description":"Keep test as-is expecting computeCount=1 (correct post-fix behavior). Test will fail now but pass after AH-001 lands. ~0 lines","effort":"0 lines"},{"id":"adjust-test-for-current-broken-state","description":"Adjust test to accept computeCount=10 for now, then revert after AH-001 fix. ~10 lines","effort":"~10 lines"}],"recommended":"keep-test-expecting-correct-behavior","attempted":["Using Effect.die for compute error","Using Ref-based counter for thread-safe counting","Verified Test 1 (success) passes correctly with count=1"]}

subject: Lane 3 complete — DuckDB Analytical Views for Runtime Events
body:
{
  "lane_id": "lane-3",
  "status": "completed",
  "waves_completed": ["learning", "plan", "execution", "validation"],
  "repair_cycles": 1,
  "claims_verified": 3,
  "files_created": [
    "packages/opencode/src/event/event-queries.ts"
  ],
  "files_modified": [
    "packages/opencode/src/storage/schema.duckdb.ts",
    "packages/opencode/src/storage/pipeline.ts",
    "packages/opencode/src/event/index.ts"
  ],
  "verification": {
    "typecheck": "pass",
    "note": "0 errors from Lane 3 files. 93 pre-existing errors from Lane 1 (event-store.ts, event-bridge.ts, runtime-event.ts) are untouched."
  },
  "blockers": [],
  "lessons": [
    "Lane 1 already created the event module (src/event/) with RuntimeEvent schema, EventStore service, Drizzle SQLite table (runtime_events), and bus bridge — integrated my DuckDB queries alongside them",
    "Effect v4 beta.66 uses `Effect.catchTag` (not `catchAll`) for error handling — important for pipeline readability"
  ],
  "recommendation": "merge",
  "details": {
    "sql_tables": [
      "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _pipeline_runtime_event (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, session_id TEXT, run_id TEXT, parent_event_id TEXT, correlation_id TEXT, ts TIMESTAMP, actor TEXT, event_type TEXT, phase TEXT, status TEXT, tool_name TEXT, file_path TEXT, model TEXT, duration_ms DOUBLE, token_input INTEGER, token_output INTEGER, error_code TEXT, error_message TEXT, recoverable BOOLEAN, payload_json TEXT) with 6 indexes"
    ],
    "sql_views": [
      "failure_hotspots — What breaks most often? Grouped by error_code, tool_name, event_type",
      "slow_tools — Which tools are slowest? Avg, median, max duration per tool with failure count",
      "file_contention — Which files touched by multiple actors? File-level multi-agent contention analysis",
      "phase_gate_denials — Which phases deny which tools? Tool-phase gate audit trail",
      "error_summary — Errors by category. Grouped by error_code, actor, tool_name with first/last seen",
      "session_timeline — Full chronological event stream per session, including correlation and parent event IDs"
    ],
    "pipeline_migration": "Schema version bumped from 1 to 2. Pipeline step 3b queries `runtime_events` table from SQLite/Postgres via DatabaseAdapter. If table doesn't exist (pre-Lane-1), catches DatabaseError and continues with empty data. Step 4b creates all analytical tables (including `_pipeline_runtime_event`). Step 5 creates all views (including the 6 new analytical views on `_pipeline_runtime_event`).",
    "query_helpers": [
      "queryFailureHotspots(db, limit?) → FailureHotspot[]",
      "querySlowTools(db, minCalls?) → SlowTool[]",
      "queryFileContention(db) → FileContention[]",
      "queryPhaseGateDenials(db) → PhaseGateDenial[]",
      "queryErrorSummary(db) → ErrorSummary[]",
      "querySessionTimeline(db, sessionId) → SessionEvent[]"
    ],
    "integration_notes": [
      "All query helpers take a DuckDBRawClient from db.duckdb.ts",
      "The pipeline loads data from Lane 1's `runtime_events` SQLite table — graceful if missing",
      "Event index.ts re-exports both Lane 1 (EventStore, RuntimeEvent, EventStoreBridge, RuntimeEventTable) and Lane 3 (query helper functions and types)",
      "No existing schema tables/views were modified — only additions"
    ]
  }
}

    "Event naming is 90% consistent (dot-notation) with 3 hyphenated exceptions — session.message.part-delta, installation.update-available, project.vcs.branch-updated",
    "Zero analytics/telemetry SDK in codebase — all structured events go through typed bus",
    "~60% of canonical taxonomy events (plugin.*, checkpoint.*, validation.*, migration.*, debug.*) don't exist yet",
    "All BusEvent.define calls have Effect Schema for payloads, but type strings are unvalidated freeform literals"
  ],
  "recommendation": "merge",
  "summary": "Complete audit of 40+ event emission sites. Found 43 unique event type strings across 25 files. 7 naming inconsistencies catalogued (3 hyphenated, 1 deprecated, 2 overlapping, 1 four-segment). 20 canonical events missing from taxonomy. Migration plan in 5 phases: fix naming → central registry → add missing events → remove deprecated → add JSON schemas."
}

subject: Lane RL-005 complete — Plugin Security Manifest + Trust Model
body:
{
  "lane_id": "RL-005",
  "status": "completed",
  "waves_completed": ["learning", "plan", "review", "execution", "validation"],
  "repair_cycles": 0,
  "claims_verified": 3,
  "files_created": ["docs/json/opencode/audits/plugin-security-manifest-audit.v1.json"],
  "files_modified": [],
  "verification": {
    "typecheck": "pass",
    "tests": "pass"
  },
  "blockers": [],
  "lessons": [
    "The plugin API grants full process access by design; any trust model must be retrofitted as a capability manifest layer without breaking existing first-party auth plugins."
  ],
  "recommendation": "merge",
  "findings_artifact": "docs/json/opencode/audits/plugin-security-manifest-audit.v1.json",
  "summary": {
    "architecture": "Mapped dual plugin system (server/CLI + desktop Electron). Server side uses Effect service with PluginLoader pipeline (resolve→load→applyPlugin→hooks). Desktop side uses DesktopPluginLoader with dynamic import in renderer process. Both share @opencode-ai/plugin SDK with 21+ lifecycle hooks, unrestricted tool registration, and zero sandboxing.",
    "security_audit": "Answered 11 adversarial questions: 5 CRITICAL (file read, prompt exfiltration, secrets access, config mutation, prompt injection), 4 HIGH (tool impersonation, UI thread locking, lifecycle bypass, fake tool registration), 1 MODERATE (event spam DOS), 1 LOW (persistence after uninstall). Root cause: hooks interface designed for trusted first-party auth providers, no adversarial model.",
    "capability_model": "Designed a 14-capability manifest schema (filesystem.read/write, network.request/server, mcp.access, tool.register/modify, event.subscribe, secrets.read, ui.slot, background.execution, autostart, shell.env, session.hooks). Each capability has scope restrictions (path globs, URL patterns, hook allowlists), display text, and risk level. Schema at rig.opencode.plugin_capability.v1.",
    "ui_display": "Designed card-based display with capability badges (7 categories), human-readable sentence format ('This plugin can read files in your project and send network requests'), detail expansion panel, risk indicators (colored borders), and per-capability toggles.",
    "implementation_priorities": "9 recommendations: P1 (manifest parsing, hook gating, tool namespace), P2 (secrets scoping, desktop sandboxing, tool.modify gate), P3 (permission prompts, rate limiting, install-time validation)."
  },
  "out_of_scope_findings_recorded": [
    "No security boundary tests exist for the plugin system",
    "PluginTransport type missing from SDK export; preload IPC transport unwired"
  ]
}

subject: Lane RL-004 complete — Secrets Handling Policy Audit
body:
{
  "lane_id": "RL-004",
  "status": "completed",
  "waves_completed": ["learning", "plan", "review", "execution", "validation"],
  "repair_cycles": 0,
  "claims_verified": 6,
  "files_created": [
    "docs/json/opencode/secrets/rl-004-findings.v1.json",
    "docs/json/opencode/plans/rl-004-secrets-policy.v1.json"
  ],
  "files_modified": [],
  "verification": {
    "artifact_validated": "pass"
  },
  "blockers": [],
  "findings_summary": {
    "existing_redaction_mechanisms": 8,
    "critical_security_gaps": 5,
    "high_risk_secret_sites": 13,
    "critical_risk_sites": 2,
    "immediate_active_leak_fixes": 2
  },
  "top_findings": [
    "sanitizedProcessEnv in packages/core/src/util/opencode-process.ts is a NO-OP — named 'sanitized' but passes ALL env vars unchanged to subprocesses",
    "createSidecarEnv in packages/desktop/src/main/server.ts:230 passes ALL env vars including OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD to the sidecar process without filtering",
    "Enterprise storage (packages/enterprise/src/core/storage.ts:67-83) reads AWS access keys/secret keys with non-null assertions — no validation, crash risk",
    "Multiple credential injection sites write secrets into process.env (AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK, AICORE_SERVICE_KEY, OPENCODE_CONSOLE_TOKEN)",
    "No ENV_BLOCKLIST, no file-read security, no bash command security, no general-purpose output scrubber exists anywhere in the codebase"
  ],
  "critical_gaps": [
    "GAP-A: sanitizedProcessEnv is a no-op (subprocess env passes ALL secrets)",
    "GAP-B: No file-read security (no binary detection, safe path, or sensitive file blocking)",
    "GAP-C: No bash command security (no rerouting, no dangerous pattern detection)",
    "GAP-D: No general-purpose output scrubber (tool output, agent messages, event payloads unprotected)",
    "GAP-E: No central ENV_BLOCKLIST utility exists"
  ],
  "policy_layers_proposed": [
    "Layer 1: Env-aware secret classifier (P0)",
    "Layer 2: ENV_BLOCKLIST for subprocesses (P0)",
    "Layer 3: Output sanitizer pipeline (P1)",
    "Layer 4: File I/O protection (P1)",
    "Layer 5: Credential lifecycle management (P2)"
  ],
  "lessons": [
    "No general-purpose secrets redaction exists in this codebase — always audit before assuming",
    "Multiple sites write credentials into process.env as an anti-pattern — use SDK constructors instead",
    "AGENTS.md describes Rig Relay security mechanisms that do NOT exist in this repo"
  ],
  "recommendation": "merge",
  "handoff_artifact": "docs/json/opencode/secrets/rl-004-findings.v1.json",
  "plan_artifact": "docs/json/opencode/plans/rl-004-secrets-policy.v1.json"
}

    "immediate_active_leak_fixes": 2,
    "data_flow_categories": ["Provider API Keys", "OAuth Tokens", "Server Password", "GitHub OAuth", "Enterprise Storage Credentials"]
  },
  "top_findings": [
    "sanitizedProcessEnv in packages/core/src/util/opencode-process.ts is a NO-OP — passes ALL env vars unchanged to subprocesses despite its name",
    "createSidecarEnv in packages/desktop/src/main/server.ts:230 passes ALL env vars including server password to sidecar process without filtering",
    "No encryption at rest: auth.json, mcp-auth.json, and SQLite account table store OAuth tokens in PLAINTEXT",
    "Console cloud functions (packages/console/) log raw secrets — OAuth tokens, API keys, Stripe webhook payloads — to stdout",
    "Enterprise storage reads AWS access keys/secret keys from process.env with non-null assertions",
    "Multiple credential injection sites write secrets back into process.env (AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK, AICORE_SERVICE_KEY, OPENCODE_CONSOLE_TOKEN)",
    "No ENV_BLOCKLIST, no file-read security, no bash command security, no general-purpose output scrubber exists anywhere"
  ],
  "critical_gaps": [
    "GAP-A: sanitizedProcessEnv is a no-op — subprocess env passes ALL secrets",
    "GAP-B: No file-read security — no binary detection, safe path, or sensitive file blocking",
    "GAP-C: No bash command security — no rerouting, no dangerous pattern detection",
    "GAP-D: No general-purpose output scrubber — tool output, agent messages, event payloads unprotected",
    "GAP-E: No central ENV_BLOCKLIST utility exists"
  ],
  "existing_good_patterns": [
    "GitHub OAuth tokens encrypted via Electron safeStorage (github-ipc.ts)",
    "HTTP recorder redaction pipeline with 7 regex patterns (http-recorder/src/redaction.ts)",
    "LLM provider error redaction (llm/src/route/executor.ts)",
    "Debug packet config key-name redaction (opencode/src/debug/debug-packet.ts)",
    "PG connection string sanitized in pool logging (opencode/src/storage/pg-pool.ts)",
    "DuckDB SQL firewall blocks dangerous operations (opencode/src/storage/duckdb-firewall.ts)",
    "Effect's Redacted type used in some paths (llm/src/route/auth.ts, database-config.ts)"
  ],
  "policy_layers_proposed": [
    "Layer 1: Env-aware secret classifier (P0)",
    "Layer 2: ENV_BLOCKLIST for subprocesses (P0)",
    "Layer 3: Output sanitizer pipeline (P1)",
    "Layer 4: File I/O protection (P1)",
    "Layer 5: Credential lifecycle management (P2)"
  ],
  "lessons_learned": [
    "No general-purpose secrets redaction exists in this codebase — always audit before assuming",
    "Multiple sites write credentials into process.env as an anti-pattern — use SDK constructors or config injection instead",
    "AGENTS.md describes Rig Relay security mechanisms (ENV_BLOCKLIST, sanitize_env_for_subprocess, etc.) that do NOT exist in this repo",
    "The only encrypted-at-rest credential storage is Electron safeStorage for GitHub tokens — all other stores (SQLite, JSON files) are plaintext",
    "Console cloud functions log raw secret values to stdout, which appears in production Cloudflare Worker logs"
  ],
  "recommendation": "merge",
  "handoff_artifact": "docs/json/opencode/secrets/rl-004-findings.v1.json",
  "plan_artifact": "docs/json/opencode/plans/rl-004-secrets-policy.v1.json"
}

subject: Lane RL-001 complete — Safe/Degraded Mode Architecture Design
body:
{
  "lane_id": "RL-001",
  "status": "completed",
  "waves_completed": ["learning", "plan", "review", "execution", "validation"],

  "blockers": [],
  "lessons": [
    "The desktop app init flow has no degraded mode — sidecar failure shows crash dialog, not Safe Mode UI",
    "PGlite init at packages/opencode/src/storage/db.pg.ts:31 has no try/catch — a corrupt data directory crashes the process",
    "MCP.defaultLayer is an Effect.effect included in AppLayer.mergeAll() — a hanging MCP server blocks the entire app runtime",
    "InitStep type has only 3 phases (server_waiting, sqlite_waiting, done) — needs extension for safe/degraded/repair phases",
    "App already has wireWindowRecovery() with Relaunch/Export Logs/Quit dialog pattern to extend"
  ],
  "recommendation": "investigate",
  "recommendation_detail": "Design artifact is complete. Recommend spawning an execution lane for Phase 1 (Init Flow Restructuring: main/index.ts, sidecar.ts, server.ts, preload/types.ts) and Phase 2 (Service Health Registry: new health.ts in opencode server). Phases 3-6 (UI, degraded indicators, session persistence, migration backup) can follow in subsequent lanes. Highest priority: wrap PGlite constructor in try/catch + add health reporting.",
  "design_artifact": "docs/json/opencode/plans/safe-degraded-mode-init.v1.json",
  "design_artifact_summary": {
    "sections": [
      "Executive Summary",
      "Current Initialization Flow Map (12-step sequence with file:line refs)",
      "9 Degradation Points Identified (critical/high/medium/low severities)",
      "Proposed 3-Tier Architecture (Safe Mode → Degraded Mode → Full Mode)",
      "6 Implementation Phases with per-file change descriptions",
      "6 Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)",
      "7 Risks Assessed with mitigations",
      "Claims Calibration (8 verified, 4 unverified)",
      "Files Summary (~17 new files, ~18 modified files, ~2,500 estimated new lines)"
    ],
    "key_design_decisions": [
      "Safe Mode runs in same renderer process (no separate window)",
      "Health registry is an Effect Service reported via /global/health",
      "Safe mode diagnosis uses static HTML/JS (no server needed)",
      "InitStep extended with detailed error phases",
      "Plugin quarantine uses persistent crash counters in config",
      "Sidecar communicates component status via typed IPC messages"
    ]
  }
}

subject: IN-003-cart complete — Evidence-Gated Push cartography
body:
{
  "lane_id": "IN-003-cart",
  "status": "completed",
  "waves_completed": ["cartography"],
  "systems_mapped": [
    "Checkpoint System (dual: Effect-based + plugin-based, no git SHA binding)",
    "Publication Record Schema (v1/v2 field drift, NO schema file exists)",
    "Verification Record Schema (NO schema file, one ad-hoc QA artifact)",
    "Prepublication Review System (3 tools, 5 verdicts, NO schema file)",
    "Git Integration / Historian (12 operations, 6 subagents, publisher agent)",
    "Lane Binders (22-field interface, but persistence is STUBBED — returns [])",
    "Claim Tracking (6-step flow, manual verification, no SHA binding)",
    "Dirty File Detection (status tracked via Git.status(), NO dirty gate anywhere)"
  ],
  "blocking_gaps": [
    "GAP-001: Three mandated schemas missing (publication_record, verification_record, prepublication_review_cycle) — no .schema.json files in docs/schemas/",
    "GAP-002: No SHA chain of custody between checkpoint creation, review cycle verdict, and pushed commit — publisher checks are procedural, not hash-verified",
    "GAP-003: No programmatic push gate evaluation — publisher agent reads artifacts manually, no tool evaluates 'can push now'",
    "GAP-005: Binder persistence is stubbed — BinderService.getBinders() returns []",
    "GAP-012: preflight_check tool does not exist — referenced in 10+ contracts/agents but no implementation, no registry entry"
  ],
  "non_blocking_gaps": [
    "GAP-004: Dual checkpoint systems not consolidated (checkpoint.ts vs prepare_checkpoint.ts)",
    "GAP-006: Publication record v1→v2 dropped 8+ fields with no schema governance",
    "GAP-007: Prepublication verdicts not freshness-gated against current code state",
    "GAP-008: No aggregate lane state computation for push gating",
    "GAP-009: Claim verification is manual only (validator agent reads code)",
    "GAP-010: git commit --amend blocked by policy only, not code",
    "GAP-011: 8 pre-existing debt findings from prior sessions",
    "GAP-013: doNotTouch field missing from Project.Info Schema — protectedFiles always empty",
    "GAP-014: Mutation tools (smart_edit, smart_write, smart_batch) have no dirty file pre-check"
  ],
  "files_created": ["docs/json/opencode/sessions/in-003/artifacts/cartography.v1.json"],
  "files_modified": [],
  "verification": {
    "typecheck": "n/a",
    "artifact_validated": true,
    "artifact_path": "docs/json/opencode/sessions/in-003/artifacts/cartography.v1.json"
  },
  "blockers": [
    "No push_gate_check tool",
    "No Schema files for 3 record types",
    "No SHA chain of custody",
    "No binder persistence",
    "No dirty file gate"
  ],
  "deferred": [
    "Detailed design of push_gate_check tool schema (for architect wave)",
    "Migration plan for publication record v1→v2 schema consolidation",
    "Lane dependency graph analysis (for cross-lane push scenarios)",
    "Red-team finding waiver schema design"
  ],
  "lessons_registered": ["evidence-gated-push-gaps", "schema-infrastructure-missing"],
  "recommendation": "Three P0 items before anything else: (1) Create the three missing JSON Schemas, (2) Implement persistent binder storage, (3) Add SHA chain-of-custody to checkpoint system. These unblock all downstream gate work."
}
